content.problemStatement:
You have a one-dimensional garden of length NNN with fountains installed at every position. The fountain at position iii has a coverage range determined by locations[i]locations[i]locations[i]. Each fountain at position iii can cover positions from max(i−locations[i],1)max(i - locations[i], 1)max(i−locations[i],1) to min(i+locations[i],N)min(i + locations[i], N)min(i+locations[i],N). Initially, all fountains are switched off. You need to find the minimum number of fountains that must be activated to cover the entire garden length.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains a single integer NNN - the length of the garden
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers locations[1],locations[2],...,locations[N]locations[1], locations[2], ..., locations[N]locations[1],locations[2],...,locations[N] - the coverage limit of each fountain




Output Format

For each test case, print a single integer representing the minimum number of fountains needed
If it's impossible to cover the entire garden, print -1

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

Fountain at position 1: covers [1,1]
Fountain at position 2: covers [1,3]
Fountain at position 3: covers [2,3] 
Only fountain at position 2 needs to be activated as it covers the entire garden [1,3]

Test Case 2:

Fountain 1: covers [1,2]
Fountain 2: covers [1,4]
Fountain 3: covers [2,4]
Fountain 4: covers [4,4]
Fountain 5: covers [4,5] 
We need minimum 2 fountains: position 2 (covers [1,4]) and position 5 (covers [4,5])
